sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम्।
यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
mad-anugrahāya paramaṁ guhyam adhyātma-saṁjñitam
yat tvayoktaṁ vacas tena moho ’yaṁ vigato mama
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: By Your grace, the supreme secret called the Self has been spoken by You; through those words my delusion is gone.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna reports that, after receiving Krishna’s personalized revelation, his cognitive fog has lifted; he now sees himself, the war, and Krishna with radical clarity.
psychological_state: Post-insight consolidation: the ‘Aha-moment’ has occurred, yet the ego seeks external confirmation to stabilize the new worldview.
trigger_scenario: A client realizes in therapy that their lifelong people-pleasing is rooted in childhood rejection; the insight feels liberating but they still need the therapist to ‘witness’ it so the old story doesn’t creep back.
anchor_text: Grace reveals the Self; delusion dissolves in its light.
